page: Microsoft Azure D2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the D server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration provides 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is set at 7.0 GB, yielding 3.5 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 102 GB, while no GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark evaluations place the D2 instance in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng CPU testing, both single-core (1245.89) and multi-core (2442.49) metrics fall into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak results, with read-only large block transfers at 23.29 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 17.26 GB/sec. Computational benchmarks reflect similar trends, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1029.0 (bottom 25%) and a multi-core score of 1896.0 (bottom 10%). Cryptographic and physics simulations via Passmark also register in the bottom 10% tier. For specialized tasks, the server achieves 2.94 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 2.77 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the D2 instance is its limited processing power and memory bandwidth, which restricts its utility for resource-intensive applications. However, because it offers dedicated CPU allocation rather than shared-core burstable resources, it provides predictable execution times for lightweight tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a low-density, entry-level tier suitable for basic utility workloads. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic database operations with small datasets, software compilation testing, and simple text or image processing tasks that do not require high-throughput parallel processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D2 is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 7.0 GB of system memory, 102 GB of local storage, and no GPU acceleration. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation but exhibits lower-tier performance across standard benchmarks. Specifically, stress-ng CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware, and memory bandwidth remains constrained. Given these performance characteristics, the D2 is best utilized for low-intensity workloads such as lightweight static web serving, basic database operations, software compilation testing, and simple text processing tasks where predictable, dedicated resource allocation is required without the need for high-throughput computing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, suitable for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2 is a general-purpose VM with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 7.0 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine with bundled local storage, designed for predictable execution of low-intensity utility workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
7.0 GB of system memory and 102 GB of bundled local storage
Dedicated CPU allocation via Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Performance profile suited for lightweight static web serving and basic database tasks
Hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, and AES instruction sets
